title: Brittney Spencer found Bob Dylan in her dressing room. Here’s what happened next.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Brittney Spencer will open for Bob Dylan in Boston at Leader Bank Pavilion on July 16.
In 2024, Dylan unexpectedly visited Spencer's dressing room after seeing her perform at Outlaw Music Fest.
Spencer joins Dylan's tour, continuing her rise after collaborations with artists like Beyoncé and Willie Nelson.

title: 5 of the best hotel restaurants in America are in New England, according to OpenTable
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: OpenTable named five New England hotel restaurants among the top 100 in America for 2026.
Featured restaurants include Contessa and Mooo Beacon Hill in Boston, both known for their distinct atmospheres.
Also recognized are Earth at Hidden Pond and White Barn Inn in Maine, and Michael Jordan’s Steak House in Connecticut.

title: Revolution’s Marko Mitrović reflected on World Cup memories
author: Sam Lattof
bullets: Marko Mitrović, head coach of New England Revolution, reflects on childhood World Cup memories starting with 1982.
Mitrović recalls Yugoslavia's 1990 World Cup match against Argentina overshadowing his aunt's wedding day.
Mitrović has coached the U.S. in international tournaments, including the 2024 Summer Olympics and 2025 FIFA U20 World Cup.

title: Joe Mazzulla showcased MMA training regimen, spoke about its influence on coaching the Celtics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Joe Mazzulla incorporates MMA training, including Brazilian jiu-jitsu with trainer Eric Nicksick, to enhance coaching the Celtics.
Mazzulla emphasizes the psychological benefits and communication insights gained from MMA in his role as a basketball coach.
Heading into his fifth season, Mazzulla aims to maintain his rigorous MMA regimen to boost the Celtics' performance.

title: Report: Wrong-way driver who killed trooper had 9 drinks before Route 1 crash
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Trooper Kevin Trainor was killed by a wrong-way driver with a blood alcohol level of 0.192.
The driver, Hernan Ramon Marrero, consumed at least nine drinks before the fatal Route 1 crash.
Marrero traveled nearly two miles in the wrong direction before colliding head-on with Trainor's cruiser.

title: The best state to move to in 2026 is in New England — and it’s not Massachusetts
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: New Hampshire ranked best state to move to in 2026, topping ConsumerAffairs' annual state ranking.
Maine and Massachusetts ranked 5th and 6th respectively in the report focusing on affordability and quality of life.
Boston's population decreased by 1,338 residents last year due to high living costs and reduced immigration.

title: Acclaimed Seaport restaurant Chickadee to close after 8 years
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Chickadee, the James Beard-nominated restaurant in Boston's Seaport, will close on July 18 after eight years.
Chef John DaSilva and Ted Kilpatrick opened Chickadee in 2018, earning accolades including a Best New Restaurant nomination.
Known for Mediterranean-inspired dishes, Chickadee adapted during the pandemic with a robust takeout program.

title: Man charged with boating under the influence after Mass. college student drowns in Connecticut
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Michael Collins charged with boating under the influence after a canoe accident on Lake Hayward, Connecticut.
Dominick Tocci, a Holy Cross student, drowned when their canoe overturned; his body was recovered after two days.
Collins turned himself in on July 10 and is set to appear in court on July 23.

title: Nine years after South End killing, fugitive wanted on murder charge captured in North Carolina
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: U.S. Marshals arrested Alan Marcus Lewis in Charlotte, North Carolina, for a 2017 Boston murder.
Lewis, accused of killing Brent Stevenson, lived under assumed identities in Houston before relocating to Charlotte.
The arrest followed a multi-year investigation by the U.S. Marshals Service and Boston Police Department.

title: The people keeping Cape Cod’s car culture alive
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Cape Cod Classics hosts over 20 car events annually, showcasing cars from a 1910 Model T to 2024 Vettes.
With nearly 500 members, the club focuses on community and charity, raising over $11,000 last year.
Heritage Museums & Gardens volunteers restored 32 of 43 cars to drivability, including a 1912 Oldsmobile Autocrat.
